free_text: Inelastic excitation of 12C and 14N by 122 MeV portons
and implications for the effective nucleon-nucleon
interaction
Quadrupole-dipole-dipole-multipole magnetic
spectrometer.
Helical-cathode position-sensitive proportional
chamber used as focal plane detector.
Two plastic scintillator detectors, placed behind the
chamber, used for particle indentification.
Relative scattering angle believed correct
to 0.1 degrees.
Uncertainty given includes statistical
uncertainties, background subtraction, and current
integration.
Tablulated data received in private communication from
J.R. Comfort, 10 November 1999.
No reply from J. Comfort to author proof.
Natural graphite targets. Areal density 11.3+10.1
mg/cm2.
Stainless steel Faraday cup internal to the scattering
chamber.
Corrected for background, dead time, and unresolved
contribution of 13C at forward angles.
Natural graphite targets. Areal density 11.3+10.1
mg/cm2.
Aluminum Faraday cup internal to the scattering
chamber.
Corrected for background, dead time, and unresolved
contribution of 13C at forward angles.

chamber.
Corrected for background, dead time, and unresolved
contribution of 13C at forward angles.

Melamine target with areal density 5.8 mg/cm2,
evaporated onto formvar and carbon backings.
Stainless steel Faraday cup internal to the scattering
chamber.
Thick BN target used to check normalization.
Corrected for background, dead time, and 12C yield
unresolved at angles less then 20 degrees.
